title: Minor head injury follow-up child

red_flags.text: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mechanism, symptoms, bleeding risk, neurological and neck documentation for child minor-head-injury follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports mechanism and time, loss of consciousness, headache, nausea or vomiting, dizziness, blurred vision, amnesia or confusion, medication and bleeding-risk review, vital signs, Glasgow Coma Scale, pupils, neurological screen, scalp, and neck documentation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury

red_flags.text: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports deterioration and neurological warning context for child minor-head-injury follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists loss of consciousness, seizure, repeated vomiting, drowsiness, worsening headache, amnesia, focal neurological or balance or speech or vision changes, skull or penetrating injury, bleeding disorder or anticoagulant use, intoxication, and non-accidental injury concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Minor Head Injury

medication.text: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.
medication.rationale: Supports observation, advice, return precautions and arranged follow-up for child minor-head-injury follow-up; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented observation, head-injury advice, analgesia decisions, worsening-symptom return precautions, and arranged follow-up without fixed routine timing or doses.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, injury date and mechanism, initial care, current symptoms, trajectory, activity, examination, imaging, diagnosis and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for child minor-head-injury follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
